title: Provisional road traffic estimates, Great Britain: April 2024 to March 2025
body: " Our statistical practice is regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing with any comments about how we meet these standards.   Provisional estimates of road traffic, by   vehicle type road class   in Great Britain for the year ending March 2025.  Provisional estimates show motor vehicles travelled 335.9 billion vehicle miles in Great Britain for the year ending March 2025. This is up 0.9% compared to the year ending March 2024. Traffic levels were 0.8% below pre-pandemic levels.  When compared to the year ending March 2024:   car traffic increased by 0.8% to 255.4 billion vehicle miles van traffic increased by 1.7% to 59.0 billion vehicle miles lorry traffic decreased by 1.2% to 16.6 billion vehicle miles traffic on motorways decreased by 0.6% to 69.9 billion vehicle miles traffic on ‘A’ roads increased by 1.4% to 148.7 billion vehicle miles traffic on minor roads increased by 1.2% to 117.3 billion vehicle miles   Contact us  Road traffic and vehicle speed compliance statistics     Email roadtraff.stats@dft.gov.uk     Media enquiries 0300 7777 878       To hear more about DfT statistical publications as they are released, follow us on X at DfTstats.  "
